Package org.apache.juneau.commons.runtime

Class Args

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.commons.runtime.Args
public class Args extends Object
Lean parser for command-line arguments passed in through a main(String[] args) method.

Supports the following grammars (all enabled by default; toggle via Args.Builder):

  • Positional arguments — any token that does not start with a recognized flag prefix.
  • Short flags — -key value (and repeated -key v1 -key v2 to collect multiple values).
  • Long flags — --key value.
  • Equals form — --key=value / -key=value.
  • System-property style — -Dkey=value (the leading D is stripped).

Positional arguments are only collected before the first flag is seen; once a flag appears, every subsequent non-flag token becomes a value of the most recently seen flag. This matches the legacy parsing rule and is preserved for backwards compatibility.

Example:

// Wrap raw command-line args. Args args = new Args(argv); // Positional access. args.get(0).ifPresent(System.out::println); // Named lookup with conversion via Optional.map(...). int port = args.get("port").map(Integer::parseInt).orElse(8080); // Multi-value flag. List<String> tags = args.getAll("tag"); // Custom grammar. Args strict = Args.create().allowEquals(false).caseSensitive(false).build(argv);

Notes:
  • This class is immutable and thread-safe once constructed.
  • get(String) returns the first value when a flag is repeated; use getAll(String) for the full list.

    Returns the number of positional arguments.

    Creates a new builder for parsing arguments with non-default grammar.

    Returns true if a positional argument exists at the given index.

    Returns true if the named option appears in the parsed args (even if no values were supplied).

  • Constructor Details

    • Args

      public Args(String line)
      Constructor that parses a raw command-line string using the default grammar.

      The string is split on whitespace honouring single- and double-quoted segments via StringUtils.splitQuoted(String).

      Parameters:
      line - The raw command line. Can be null (treated as an empty argv).

    • Args

      public Args(String[] argv)
      Constructor that parses an argv array using the default grammar.
      Parameters:
      argv - The arguments passed in through a main(String[] args) method. Can be null (treated as an empty argv).

  • Method Details

    • create

      public static Args.Builder create()
      Creates a new builder for parsing arguments with non-default grammar.
      Returns:
      A new builder.

    • get

      public Optional<String> get(int index)
      Returns the positional argument at the specified zero-based index.
      Parameters:
      index - The position.
      Returns:
      The argument value, or empty if index is out of range.

    • get

      public Optional<String> get(String key)
      Returns the first value of the named option, or empty if the option was not set.

      If the option was specified multiple times (e.g. -tag a -tag b), this returns only the first value. Use getAll(String) to get the full list.

      Parameters:
      key - The option name (without the flag prefix).
      Returns:
      The first value, or empty if the key is unset or has no values.

    • getAll

      public List<String> getAll(String key)
      Returns all values for the named option in the order they appeared on the command line.
      Parameters:
      key - The option name (without the flag prefix).
      Returns:
      All values, or an empty list if the key is unset.

    • positional

      public List<String> positional()
      Returns the positional arguments in the order they appeared on the command line.
      Returns:
      An unmodifiable list of positional arguments.

    • argCount

      public int argCount()
      Returns the number of positional arguments.
      Returns:
      The count.

    • optionCount

      public int optionCount()
      Returns the number of distinct named options.
      Returns:
      The count.

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Returns true if no positional arguments and no named options were captured.
      Returns:
      true if both axes are empty.

    • has

      public boolean has(int index)
      Returns true if a positional argument exists at the given index.
      Parameters:
      index - The zero-based index.
      Returns:
      true if an argument exists at that index.

    • has

      public boolean has(String key)
      Returns true if the named option appears in the parsed args (even if no values were supplied).
      Parameters:
      key - The option name.
      Returns:
      true if the option was set.

    • asMap

      public Map<String,List<String>> asMap()
      Returns the named-option contents as an unmodifiable map.

      Positional arguments are not included — use positional() for those.

      Returns:
      An unmodifiable map of named options to their value lists.
